_note: 2026-07-11 hardening-audit batch: 17 investigation specs (docs deep-dive + session mining) enqueued at the head in recommended fix order — enforcement re-arm first (the settings split-brain un-blinds telemetry and re-arms every guard), then the efficacy-loop pair (so later fixes get graded), then state-plane crash consistency, pipeline-waste killers, and hygiene. HEAD: the P0 'skip-mcp-test-frontmatter-unquoted-colon' hardening bug is the merged-view head — /lazy-batch's unified driver picks it FIRST. The 10 build-queue/harness bugs below it carry a DEPRIORITIZED queue-entry severity of null (real severity lives in each SPEC's **Severity:** line) so they sort to merged priority 99 — AFTER every feature — set 2026-07-04 (resume-lanes-hardening) because they are PowerShell/Pester build-queue defects untestable off a Windows workstation and were otherwise interleaving ahead of the feature queue in the merged view. The two adhoc-* bugs were already null-severity. Restore a bug's priority by removing its queue entry (autodiscovery re-reads the SPEC severity) or setting an explicit severity here.
